reviews.review_msg: Hardest teacher I have ever taken. It was horrible, the highest grade was a 77% which in an intro class is ridiculous. It is in an intro class this should not be that hard. As someone who is just starting out at school again, this was so discouraging and not even useful in any course. Do not take it if you can avoid it.

Carl Peters is the worst teacher I've ever had! He constantly goes off topic and doesn't have a clear grading scale He talks about himself nonstop when he should be teaching. He is biased on grading, if he disagrees with your thesis prepare to fail your essay. I don't know why he became a teacher when it seems like his sole purpose is to fail you!

Carl likes the experimental stuff, which is refreshing at UFV. The texts are difficult, but valuable. Carl also likes to talk, but what he has to say is usually extremely interesting. He does not put up with dismissive attitudes. His lectures can be confusing, but he will meet outside of class to help and recommend further, wilder reading.

Carl focused more on his own writing work than helping the students to be better writers. The lectures were not helpful and I found it hard to stay focused without any class interaction. Essays needed to be what he wanted to hear and different opinions were given lower grades. I would not take a class of his again.

I was so excited to take this class but I feel as though I learned next to nothing. Carl means well and he is genuinely interested in what he's doing but he's just not well grounded. I am now considering switching to a Psych major because of this class.
A genius.
I still have nightmares about my experience years later.  If you see Carl coming your way, RUN in the other direction!!!
Don't lose sight that English, and the works that are studied is art.  All those not interested can stick to their Cole's Notes.
Carl is brutal. If he wants to subvert the system, great, but he shouldn't do so when people are paying money to be there. Oh well, now he can fight the unemployment system!

super knowledgeable about art and connection of visual arts to literature!  I was intimidated and he was hard to write a paper for, but my insight into the material was rewarded.  for carl, literature is allied to the fine arts...knowing this helps

Tried really hard to like Carl, but he's so scattered and incomprehensible. Will also avoid you if you 'pester' him for constructive feedback. Grading system nonsensicial and many students took their concerns to the dept.
absolutely a genius!  I have never met a man with such intelligence and shine.  By far the best prof SFU will ever have.  I have never learned so much in my entire life
I am disappointed with those of you disrupting this highly scientific rating system. Like his degree, Carl needs to purchase a personality.
Carl lacks, as do his heroes Stein and bp!
YES. makes students think - crazy i know - but you learn because you HAVE to - doesn't spoon feed his ideas or anything - EXCELLENT.
I struggle with the material, but hey, so does he.  (You can't rate such a brilliant, colourful mind)

i thought he was great until he marked papers. Very vague in what he asks for and if you dont like pollack or db nichols or pearl jam don't bother going to class- also big on hand outs at the end of the term i had 3 booklets full of this crap
Carl is great. Brilliant. One of my best profs ever.
Incredible. He's brilliant! That says it best! Thanx fr a great class, Carl!
This guy marks hard if you try to make a good paper, best way to an A, use big words that he might not understand and then try to write a paper that is as intangable as his lectures.
This guy makes no sense, except maybe if you're high! If you don't like structure, go for him, if you want someone to say something tangeble, stay away from him!!!
Philosophical, poetic. Brilliant.
he's a very skilled associational thinker, as the plethora of overlapping, multicoloured, rotating overheads (most of which involved william s. burroughs and a shotgun) would indicate.  curious.
Carl is amazing and his class changed my life
uhhh.. very hard. u dont know where u stand with him
No Comments
very passionate teacher
Brillant Prof.  Off the wall
